summary: Enhanced Auto-Create List Functionality
description_text: Current State: Currently on XPro 1.0, users have the ability to auto-create lists. To do so, the user must send in D messages and utilize the auto-create functionality to automatically create an RFQ list Future State: In XPro 2.0, the purpose is to create a more robust offering of this feature that includes the following parameters: List Type (Bin or ASAP) With these list types, we want to have the following sub-parameters available to drive the creation of lists Product Trading Protocol Liquidity Score Auto-X Eligibility Side (Buy or Sell) Example Scenarios (note - not limited to these cases) Input #1 Example: User has six D messages of the following products: Items: Item 1: US High Yield (default trading protocol: price, auto-x eligible) Item 2: Domestic Corp (default trading protocol: spread, auto-x ineligible) Item 3: Emerging Markets Price (default trading protocol: price, auto-x eligible) Item 4: Emerging Markets Price/Emerging Markets Spread Crossover (trading protocol set to spread on D message, auto-x eligible) Item 5: Muni Taxable (trading protocol set to spread on D message, auto-x eligible) Item 6: EU High Yield (default trading protocol: price, auto-x eligible) Case 1 Input:  User selects the following Bin Product: Emerging Markets Price, Emerging Markets Spread, US High Yield Auto-X Eligible only Output:  Auto-create a bin list with two items (Item 1 US High Yield and Item 4 Emerging Markets Price/Spread Crossover) Item 3 Emerging Markets Price (non-crossover bond) not in this list because it does not satisfy Auto-X requirement Item 2 Domestic Corp, Item 5 Muni Taxable, and Item 6 EU High Yield not in this list because they do not satisfy Product requirement Case 2 Input:  User selects the following ASAP Product: Emerging Markets Price, EU High Yield, Domestic Corp Output:  Auto-create an ASAP list with three items (Item 2 Domestic Corp, Item 3 Emerging Markets Price, and Item 6 EU High Yield) Item 1 High Yield not in this list because they do not sati…

summary: [QA] Enable Partial List submission - (US RFQ)
description_text: User story As a client, when I submit or build lists that may contain a mix of eligible and ineligible items across EU Price, USHG/USHB, EM and Global ASAP, I want the system to submit only eligible items in a new list so that EU/US/EM price lists are converted to Global ASAP when configured and eligible. As a client, I want ineligible items to remain on the original list in RTS state so that mixed USHG / High Yield / USHB / EM lists have ineligible items dropped and only eligible items submitted. As a client, I want single-item and build-list flows to follow the same rules so that lists are never left in an invalid state due to partially eligible content. Context This issue addresses the need for the system to handle submissions of lists that contain both eligible and ineligible items. It focuses on ensuring that only eligible items are submitted while maintaining the integrity of the original list. Acceptance criteria The system must submit only eligible items in a new list. Ineligible items must remain on the original list in RTS state. The system must route to the correct list type: EU Price, US Price, Global ASAP, Multi-Market. EU/US/EM price lists must be converted to Global ASAP when configured and eligible. Mixed USHG / High Yield / USHB / EM lists must drop ineligible items and submit only eligible items. Single-item and build-list flows must adhere to the same rules. Other information Configuration settings involved:  feature.EnableGlobalASAPInXPro and  feature.forceToGlobalASAPInXpro both should be Y

summary: [FE] Display Spreadsheet uploader based on user permission
description_text: In linx there is a user level preference “Restrict User to RTS Only”. When this is ticked, a user is prevented from submitting an unsolicited RFQ. When it is not ticked, then a user can submit an unsolicited RFQ. In XPro, this will mean the following: If a user is enabled with “Restrict User to RTS Only“, then the spreadsheet uploader component should not be visible to a Trader If a user is  NOT  enabled with “Restrict User to RTS Only“, then the spreadsheet uploader component should be visible to the Trader AND they can submit an unsolicited RFQ

summary: [BE] Spot Negotiation Workflow
description_text: Business Background 1-step can fail due to several reasons including failure to receive a spot price, failed hedge or a non-calculatable bond. If a 1-step fails, the trade will transition to a Done - XXX state where the dealer must take action/provide a spot and trade price. Once the dealer submits the spot and trade price, the client is then required to take action to accept or reject.

summary: [FE] Spot Negotiation Workflow
description_text: Business Background 1-step can fail due to several reasons including failure to receive a spot price, failed hedge or a non-calculatable bond. If a 1-step fails, the trade will transition to a Done - XXX state where the dealer must take action/provide a spot and trade price. Once the dealer submits the spot and trade price, the client is then required to take action to accept or reject.

summary: [QA] Spot Negotiation Workflow
description_text: Business Background If a spread trade fails to spot, the trade will transition into a spot negotiation workflow. The workflow will allow the dealer to submit a spot price and final price for the client to accept or reject bringing the trade to a Done state.

Context This issue addresses the need for the system to handle submissions of lists that contain both eligible and ineligible items. Ineligible items must remain on the original list in RTS state. The system must route to the correct list type: EU Price, US Price, Global ASAP, Multi-Market. summary: XPro 2.0: For EU Products – Other dealer inquiry remains in Live state instead of Subject when one dealer inquiry is Passed/Timeout; Countering columns not displayed
description_text: For EU product inquiries, when one dealer inquiry moves to Passed or Timeout state after countering, the other dealer’s responded inquiry remains in Live state in the Dealer Response Grid instead of transitioning to Subject state. As a result, countering columns are not displayed for the other dealer while it remains in Live state. The client is unable to counter until the inquiry status transitions to Subject state. Other dealer responded inquiries should automatically move to Subject state once another dealer inquiry reaches Passed or Timeout state, and countering columns should be available accordingly. Steps to Reproduce: Client submits the single or list on EU products from XPRO 2.0 to two or more than two dealers. (Dealer A and dealer B). All dealers respond with different levels. Client counters with  Dealer A. Dealer A passed inquiry or Dealer A don’t take any actions (TimeOut) Let Dealer A inquiry move to Passed or Timeout state. Observe Dealer B inquiry status in Dealer Response Grid. Issue :  Dealer B inquiry remains in Live state. It does not automatically move to  Subject  state. Countering columns are not displayed for Dealer B while it remains in Live state. Client is unable to counter until Dealer B inquiry transitions to Subject state. For Passed inquiry For Timeout inquiry:

summary: [Playwright] Build automated migration tools for Ranorex to Playwright BDD conversion
description_text: Build a suite of automation tools to streamline migration of 1000+ Ranorex C# tests to Playwright BDD. Deliverables: Pattern Library - Define all BDD patterns and anti-patterns Ranorex Parser (migrate:parse) - Analyze C# tests and classify migration strategy Prompt Generator (migrate:generate-prompt) - Generate AI prompts with embedded patterns Pattern Validator ( migrate:validate ) - Validate migrated tests for quality Documentation (WORKFLOW.md, MIGRATION_GUIDE.md, README.md) Value: Provides repeatable workflow with automated parsing, AI generation, and pattern validation to reduce migration time and ensure consistency across 1000+ test migrations.

summary: [BE] Targeted RFQ mapping for min size
description_text: We use the system properties to control the min size to enable/disable TargetedRFQ on the UI the used property is TargetedRFQMinZize.<Value> → the original approach <value> was defined as marketsegment description, but seems like the current configuration in those propertis are not matching only the marketsegment description, but also the product code. Here is a table showing the matching GBP_GOV    -> match EURO_COV   -> match EURO_HY    -> won't match EUR_HY is defined although EURO_HY in APIName dec, also a product EURO_GBP   -> match EMDS       -> won't match, not even market segment, but product EUR_GOV    -> match EUEM       -> won't match, not even market segment, but product EUR_HY     -> match USEM       -> won't match, but product match EURO_EUR   -> match EURO_FRN   -> match JPY_BOND   -> match EURO_SAS   -> match BRYN       -> ?? no market segment, no product, is this in prod? USHG       -> won't match, but product match EURO_USD   -> match NON_CORE   -> match (I would bet it won't :) ) EURO_NONCORE   -> ?? not market segment or product The proposal solution is to match product code or marketsegment description, that will cover all the remaining options except by BRYN and EURO_NONCORE (they are not present in production)

summary: [QA] improve even more transient states
description_text:
as part of a previous ticket we have renamed transient status fields so they no longer override current inquiry/list status. then UI applied a table if weight to define which one to use. { status: '', weight: 0 },
  { status: 'CREATING', weight: 0.5 },
  { status: 'DELISTING', weight: 0.5 },
  { status: 'OPEN', weight: 1 },
  { status: 'SUBMITTING', weight: 2 },
  { status: 'CANCELLING', weight: 2 },
  { status: 'LIVE', weight: 3 },
  { status: 'TRYING', weight: 4 },
  { status: 'TRADING', weight: 4 },
  { status: 'PASSING', weight: 4 },
  { status: 'COMPLETING', weight: 4 },
  { status: 'COUNTERING', weight: 4 },
  { status: 'PASSED', weight: 5 },
  { status: 'COMPLETE', weight: 5 },
  { status: 'CANCELLED', weight: 5 }, this is the revised version where creating and delisting are less than open otherwise we can never go back to open. What this ticket aims is to fix that. Plan is to add transientStatus timestamp from transient state and statusTimestamp from singleRow service (in a new topic/schema to avoid disruption) So ui can resolve CREATING/DELISTING vs OPEN weight draw with the timestamp.

summary: [Canon] Refactor AutoXData schema to support protocol‑specific Auto-X details
description_text: Background   -  In order to support crossover bonds, based on design discussions between Xpro BE, Canonical and Architecture (Ashish), we’ve agreed that we’ll create aggregated/fat objects for Distribution, InquiryData, Validations and AutoXData that would contain detail for all the supported protocols. This means that only the repeating fields are added as part of new sub-object (AutoXDetailByProtocol.avsc?) and the non-repeating fields remain in the original object - AutoXData.avsc (see comments on proposed change). Along the same lines, changes to Distribution, InquiryData objects to include details per protocol are handled as part of this PR for reference -  https://bitbucket.vip.marketaxess.com/projects/MKTX/repos/mktx-data/pull-requests/368/diff Create protocol-wise Auto-X details in the integration server. This issue involves modifications to the existing  AutoXData  Avro file and the creation of a new Avro file.

summary: [BE] 26.1.0 MR3 vulnerability fixes
description_text: User story As a developer, when I scan the Docker image, I want to identify vulnerabilities so that I can ensure the security of our application. Context This issue addresses vulnerabilities found in the Docker image  mktxv-docker-prod-virtual.artifacts.tools.marketaxess.com/xpro-adapter-rfq-eu-grouped-row:1.0.209566 . The scan results indicate a high severity vulnerability related to Oracle Java SE. Acceptance criteria Address the identified vulnerabilities, specifically: CVE-2026-21932 Severity: High CVSS: 7.40 Package: Java Version: 21.0.9 Status: Fixed in versions 22.0.1, 18, 12.0.0 Description: Vulnerability in the Oracle Java SE, Oracle GraalVM for JDK, Oracle GraalVM Enterprise Edition product of Oracle Java SE (component: AWT, JavaFX). Other information N/A

summary: [BE] Prompt to trace service execution via static analysis.
description_text: After making a prompt to analyze our services it has gotten some interest from other BE/FE engineers as a way to trace service execution of simulated event input. This could be thought of as sitting at the intersection between testing at runtime and documentation: Like at runtime:  the output is dependent on the “events” our services are ingesting/emitting. Like documentation:  the output includes flow diagrams and summary statements about what each service does. Unlike at runtime:  no code is executed. Unlike documentation:  the output includes details on what the services do for a precise scenario which can be specified ad hoc (which the prompt walks the user through setting up). Currently the prompt: Does an initial scan to learn available services and asks user to make some initial choices about service to start with. Scans selected service to learn topology and inbound/outbound events. Asks user to specify any number of the inbound event types the service supports.  It will pick reasonable default field values with the option to override so user can fine tune their input scenario. Asks user to optionally specify time staggering details so user can simulate events arriving fast/slow/some combination thereof. Analyzes our source to determine the effect this input has on the system and generates a report (flow diagram and verbage detailing what happens with topologies and the values inside events as they are produced/consumed) Does some rudimentary caching (currently implemented as a JSON file) to avoid some of the scanning on subsequent runs (and will inform user of this and ask if the cache should be rebuilt which is useful if the user changes any source). From feedback it seems there is a desire to have it: Include context from canonical Include context from mongo/viewserver. Include context from the UI. Have options to specify input at the UI layer and see the effect upstream and down. Improve caching to include any learning done after the initial step (curren…

summary: [BE] Get BondLink Calcs Necessary for Aggregations
description_text: In order to get list analytics, we first need to get bondlink calcs for all inquries in a list (accrued interests, sink factors, estimated prices/yields/spreads/gspreads). Create a BE microservice that uses HTTP → JMS so we can send messages from EKS to on-prem calc service. Implement a punctuator in the backend that batches inquiries to be calced every second and performs batched calc requests. Perform calcs again if benchmark, settlement date, price, etc changes. Output calcs to mongo collection. Join calced data with inquiry/instrument data to get all data points necessary for aggregations

summary: [BE] Send Holidays to the Atlas Collection.
description_text: We need to add Holidays to xpro for the settlement date pickers to grey out the holidays as expected.  The Approach: New Service that reads from kafka on  mktx.trading.xpro.rfq._protected.xpro.holidayrecord Get rid of holidays that might be beyond 1 years. Add the key which is calendar ID to the avro schema write into a output topic, and send it to a new collection called  xpro-holidays  or something like that. The number of records in the holidays collection should match what is in the  util_calendar Also: Add calendar ID to the enriched instrument topic  mktx.trading.xpro.refdata_protected.instrument-enriched  from the  MarketAxess.BondLink.External.Public.Instrument.ProductInstrument UI Will use this as follows: On opening the date picker: take calendar ID from the rfq instrument collection, and query the  xpro-holidays  collection to get your holidays as a array

summary: [BE] Test Automation Ticket
description_text: As a  squad lead , I want  an automated refinement check on Jira issues moved to “Ready for Dev”  so that  stories below an 80% quality threshold are automatically bounced back to “Backlog” with a clear scoring breakdown. System behaviour ·       When an issue is transitioned to “Ready for Dev”, an automation rule runs a scoring agent against seven refinement categories. ·       The automation posts a comment with a markdown table:  Category \| Score \| Rationale . ·       If the total score is < 80% or any disqualifying zero is present, the issue is moved back to “Backlog.” Business rationale ·       Reduce wasted dev time by ensuring only sufficiently refined work enters the sprint. ·       Standardise refinement quality across teams with a visible, consistent scorecard.

summary: CLONE - [FE] improve even more transient states
description_text: The 2 points here has been split in half from the parent

summary: [FE] improve even more transient states
description_text:
as part of a previous ticket we have renamed transient status fields so they no longer override current inquiry/list status. then UI applied a table if weight to define which one to use. { status: '', weight: 0 },
  { status: 'CREATING', weight: 0.5 },
  { status: 'DELISTING', weight: 0.5 },
  { status: 'OPEN', weight: 1 },
  { status: 'SUBMITTING', weight: 2 },
  { status: 'CANCELLING', weight: 2 },
  { status: 'LIVE', weight: 3 },
  { status: 'TRYING', weight: 4 },
  { status: 'TRADING', weight: 4 },
  { status: 'PASSING', weight: 4 },
  { status: 'COMPLETING', weight: 4 },
  { status: 'COUNTERING', weight: 4 },
  { status: 'PASSED', weight: 5 },
  { status: 'COMPLETE', weight: 5 },
  { status: 'CANCELLED', weight: 5 }, this is the revised version where creating and delisting are less than open otherwise we can never go back to open. What this ticket aims is to fix that. Plan is to add transientStatus timestamp from transient state and statusTimestamp from singleRow service (in a new topic/schema to avoid disruption) So ui can resolve CREATING/DELISTING vs OPEN weight draw with the timestamp.

summary: [FE] Abort previous list requests for AC/RFQ/PT
description_text: Afer analyzing Prod issue DataDog logs and session replay ( https://app.datadoghq.com/rum/replay/sessions/55687dae-c790-436d-b5e4-42884a7b7347?applicationId=c96759f3-ce8a-4e2c-81dc-25c1acbe936b&graphType=flamegraph&p_devtools_tab=performance&seed=9c8b9956-6b4d-43b3-af63-4cbe3364a27f&shouldShowLegend=true&traceQuery=&ts=1769711399706&from=1769711399498 ). We came to conclusion that user’s RFQ list request is very slow because the browser takes time to download 3.6 Mb list and it depends on internet speed however when user is requesting to update list multiple times we are doing multiple downloads in parallel and overall bandwidth is considerably slowed. Since every list update is latest data we can safely abort all previous list requests and leave only latest. Best way to do it is using AbortController. However we also need to add a bit of safety so that if there are list updates coming all the time we still need to allow some of them to be processed without aborting. For that we decided to abort N consecutive list requests and allow N+1 to be processed.

summary: [FE/BE] Decouple dealer response stack from row builder data
description_text: EXISTING FLOW: When client clicks trade, UI auto-selects next row → rhp/inquiry call will fetch full list of dealer responses, normalize them onto the inquiry, and fire REFRESH_TAB WS message → UI will fire a REST call to fetch latest row state from cache to display full set of dealer responses. SOLUTION: Decouple the dealer responses from the inquiry row builders, and have a separate REST endpoint to fetch the full list of dealers, separate from REFRESH_TAB events.

summary: [FE] Abort previous RFQ list requests
description_text: Afer analyzing Prod issue DataDog logs and session replay ( https://app.datadoghq.com/rum/replay/sessions/55687dae-c790-436d-b5e4-42884a7b7347?applicationId=c96759f3-ce8a-4e2c-81dc-25c1acbe936b&graphType=flamegraph&p_devtools_tab=performance&seed=9c8b9956-6b4d-43b3-af63-4cbe3364a27f&shouldShowLegend=true&traceQuery=&ts=1769711399706&from=1769711399498 ). We came to conclusion that user’s RFQ list request is very slow because the browser takes time to download 3.6 Mb list and it depends on internet speed however when user is requesting to update list multiple times we are doing multiple downloads in parallel and overall bandwidth is considerably slowed. Since every list update is latest data we can safely abort all previous list requests and leave only latest. Best way to do it is using AbortController. However we also need to add a bit of safety so that if there are list updates coming all the time we still need to allow some of them to be processed without aborting. For that we decided to abort N consecutive RFQ list requests and allow N+1 to be processed.

summary: Latency around REFRESH_TAB flow
description_text: Problem User complained in production while trading down the stack of inquiries in his list that there is a large delay in displaying all dealer responses to the selected inquiry. This user had several large lists with what looks like 30+ responses per inquiry item. This means there is increased traffic on the UI that most likely resulted in the latency this user experienced. Replay Session Investigation Update Post debugging what we found is, The issue arises from parallel  /tab/list  REST calls from the UI. When these calls are made simultaneously, the network bandwidth is shared, causing slower response times. Traders are experiencing delays during continuous REFRESH_TAB events. Ex:  If a single call takes 5 seconds for resolving the REST call + receiving/transferring the response over n/w, 4 parallel calls take 20 seconds each. This is exactly what traders have been experiencing when there are continuous REFRESH_TAB events from BE. We also found that the  UNKNOWN  time which Datadog shows is the time the client takes to download all the response over the network (this varies by n/w bandwidth). We reproduced the same locally as well where the REST call returns 200 but the client still resolves it until it downloads all the data. Note:  When you trade line by line by clicking on Buy/Sell on each item, BE sends 1 REFRESH_TAB for transient state, and one REFRESH_TAB will come as UI auto-selects the next line time, and there will be another REFRESH_TAB event for the traded one coming from bondlnk Solutions Abort all the parallel /tab/list calls excep the current one, this will make the current call much faster. For EBF, scope is to apply this logic only for RFQ lists to minimize the risk -    We can apply this across the board for AC, RFQ & PT - Don’t send REFRESH_TAB event when client clicks Buy or Sell on list item inquiry, this will reduce one REFRESH_TAB event Dcouple the dealer responses from the inquiry row builders, and have a separate REST endpoint to fetch t…

description_text: Bin lists are not filtered out in XPro 2.0 (autosubmitted as  BIN lists from Suffix or initiated from WS manually) Steps to reproduce (WS): In WS click Trade List Uncheck ASAP and submit new list as a BIN list Check XPro 2.0 (old designs or new designs) Actual result: new BIN list is shown in XPro 2.0, but should not Same list in WS: In XPro 2.0: Steps to reproduce (FIX): Send EU list as Holding BIN with  autosubmit  from Suffix: e.g. 8=FIX.4.4|9=289|35=D|34=212|49=ABAX|52=20260128-17:56:31.969|56=MA|11=CLORDID10497|453=1|448=ct4_abaxuk2|447=C|452=11|21=2|55=NA|48=XS1652855815|22=4|54=2|60=20260128-17:56:31.969|38=300000|40=1|423=1|20002=2|9690=2|9691=DEUTDEFF|7762=Y|9692=B|9691=BARXUS|7762=Y|9692=B|6886=180|9597=ClientRTSBook|5625=2|10=105| Check the list in WS and in XPro 2.0 Actual result: autosubmitted BIN list is shown in WS and in XPro 2.0 Send EU list as Holding BIN for  manual  submission from Suffix 8=FIX.4.4|9=289|35=D|34=212|49=ABAX|52=20260128-17:56:31.969|56=MA|11=CLORDID10497|453=1|448=ct4_abaxuk2|447=C|452=11|21=3|55=NA|48=XS1652855815|22=4|54=2|60=20260128-17:56:31.969|38=400000|40=1|423=1|20002=2|9690=2|9691=DEUTDEFF|7762=Y|9692=B|9691=BARXUS|7762=Y|9692=B|6886=180|9597=ClientRTSBook|5625=2|10=105| Check the list in WS and in XPro 2.0 => Fix initiated list is shown in WS and XPro 2.0 ready for manual submission -  expected . As if the list will be submitted from XPro 2.0, it will be converted to Global ASAP list. Open the list in WS and submit it as BIN list, check XPro 2.0 Actual result: BIN list still shown in XPro 2.0, but should be filtered out Expected result: Fix initiated list (Holding Bin) appears in XPro 2.0, can be submitted from XPro 2.0 and converted to Global ASAP list.  If the list is submitted from WS, or autosubmitted from fix as BIN list, it should be hidden from XPro 2.0.

summary: [FE] Viewserver Integration Refactor
description_text: The Platform UI team has provided a new approach (at the time of the creation of this ticket) to integrate grids with the view server. Instead of using opaque stateful components that pull data directly, we will use hooks that will provide AG Grid props and instantiate grids directly. This is a much more transparent, simple pattern to implement grids. It also yields a much simpler testing and mocking approach. We would like to refactor all grids to use this approach. We would also like to refactor testing around this, as testing and test mocks will be considerably simpler as a result.

summary: [QA][Core 2 Dependency] Sanity test Global ASAP workflow in 26.1.2
description_text: As per Core 2: Moment fee integration We are looking to work on an enhancement in CTAPI for our Vendor Moment, where the Companies sending in inquiries via Moment will be enabled with a new Company property and will send in two new FIX tags (Mostly Tag 12 and Tag 13) in their D/E messages. If Client is enabled with the new Company property and the two FIX tags are identified in the inbound D/E messages, Moment fee will be sent back them in their original AI message in CTAPI and ER in RTS TAPI session. We would like teams to run a basic sanity on their CTAPI workflows to ensure no impact. Epic link: Market segments: All US Price (IG on Price, HY, EM Price, IG Corp, Muni Tax-exempt) Release: 26.1.2, Sprint 4 Impacted Teams: Core 3 and 4/5/XPro ASAP, Munis and Automation

summary: [BE] Dealer Response Levels Service does not update Best Vs calcs when best dealer withdraws/cancels
description_text: I have 2 state stores after the joins, 1 keyed by responseid and 1 by inquiry id (for best calcs). Seems  each response is in a separate partition with own state store for best diff keyed by inquiryid. So when you respond with 2 dealers and then the best dealer cancels. The partition of that response can recalculate the new diff with second best dealer but it compares that with what was in THAT PARTITION's state store and it is the same so no emission to mongo.

summary: [Canonical] RFM Post-Submission/Response Changes (MCP Generated)
description_text: Canonical: RFM Post-Submission/Response Changes Notes from Monday.com: 1. Grid shows Bid/Offer stacks --> show Side=Market in stack in same grid logic as when Side = Buy show Offer Stack / Sell show Bid Stack (even though no execution/counter allowed on RFM, client needs to know if dealer is willing to provide two-sided prices, this is to inform execution follow on) 2. Counter and Fill buttons should not be enabled when Side=Market 3. From an RFM (and only RFM) allow for client to CHANGE direction of their quote request (change from Side = Market to Buy or Sell)  4. Allow for client to execute on an RFM quote via one of the fill buttons (when quote is not yet expired and executing dealer is one of the dealers on the disclosed list). Once a client changes from Side = Market direction to Bid or Sell, the RFM is submitted to ONLY the selected dealer (other dealer responses are no longer shown and dealer order may change)

Dev Requirements Column Changes: For OMS Submitted Lists Security → ListID (value sent through tag 66 on E message) OrderID → Blank List name → ListID (value sent through tag 66 on E message) ListID → InquiryListID For Lists Created through Create List Security → MKTX generated list name (typically My List X) OrderID → Blank List name → MKTX generated list name (typically My List X) ListID → InquiryListID For OMS Submitted Singles Security → Instrument short name details (ex: GM 6.000 1/9/28 c27) OrderID → Client Order ID (value sent through Tag 11 on D message) List Name → Blank ListID → Blank Detailed Panel Tab Changes: For OMS submitted lists Tab name → List Name For Lists Created through Create List Tab name → List Name For Single Orders opened as Individual Order and Individual Order within List Tab Name → Security Column Info List Tab on Detailed Panel

summary: [Core 1 Dependency (Stretch)] Disabling OT on US Spread Sanity
description_text: Background on Change Core 1 are adding a new system property to disable Open Trading only on US Spread protocol (including Muni taxable and EM spread). This would be an intraday updatable property. Test to ensure Open Trading is disabled for US Spread protocol when the property ‘inquiry.DisableOT.Spread' is set to Y for their workflows. We also request rest of the teams to sanity test Open Trading workflows for their protocols and ensure there is no impact when the system property is set to Y.

summary: [Core 1 Dependency] Net Hedge Controls per Tenor Testing
description_text: Background on Change Core 1 are adding system properties to control which benchmark would hedge manually for a specific spot time. The property will be called something like ‘inquiry.NetHedge.XX.YY’, where XX stands for Benchmark like 10YC and YY stands for spot time like 4PM. When a property is set to M, that specific BM will hedge manually (Done-AHC flow) at a specific time. And if it is set to A, then it will go through the rates orchestrator (gets hedged automatically). We request teams to sanity test this functionality for their hedging workflows.

summary: [Core 1 Dependency] Aladdin Including Logic Sanity in 2.0 for Fix-Function-DealerIncludeOnly
description_text: Background on Dependency PTM sessions enabled with Fix-Function-DealerIncludeOnly by default will have include only behavior on inclusions (similar to having 20002=1 but without this tag). This will coincide with company property inquiry.RTS_ItemLevelDealerSelection to support inclusions per line item. The change is to ensure MKTX dealer will only be included if included intentionally. Changes are across all market segments.

User select the Due in and Good For Timer as more than 1min and submit  Example:  Due in 2mins, Good For 2mins User should submit the Direct trade from model pop up by clicking submit button Expected Once the bin timer starts, Due in time should decrease from 2 minute And once the response is received from the dealer after bin released  Good for Timer should decrease from 2 minute Actual Due in and Good for taking 1min by default,  if the user select the  timer as more than 1min also , Due in time  and Good for timer decrease from 60 seconds

summary: [BE] improve even more transient states
description_text:
as part of a previous ticket we have renamed transient status fields so they no longer override current inquiry/list status. then UI applied a table if weight to define which one to use. { status: '', weight: 0 },
  { status: 'CREATING', weight: 0.5 },
  { status: 'DELISTING', weight: 0.5 },
  { status: 'OPEN', weight: 1 },
  { status: 'SUBMITTING', weight: 2 },
  { status: 'CANCELLING', weight: 2 },
  { status: 'LIVE', weight: 3 },
  { status: 'TRYING', weight: 4 },
  { status: 'TRADING', weight: 4 },
  { status: 'PASSING', weight: 4 },
  { status: 'COMPLETING', weight: 4 },
  { status: 'COUNTERING', weight: 4 },
  { status: 'PASSED', weight: 5 },
  { status: 'COMPLETE', weight: 5 },
  { status: 'CANCELLED', weight: 5 }, this is the revised version where creating and delisting are less than open otherwise we can never go back to open. What this ticket aims is to fix that. Plan is to add transientStatus timestamp from transient state and statusTimestamp from singleRow service (in a new topic/schema to avoid disruption) So ui can resolve CREATING/DELISTING vs OPEN weight draw with the timestamp.

summary: [FE][QA] Auto-Dismiss IOI Toast Notification on User Acknowledgment
description_text: Enhance the IOI toast notification system to automatically dismiss notifications when users take actions that implicitly acknowledge the IOI message. This will improve user experience by keeping notifications relevant and prevent users from attempting to trade already-actioned inquiries. Improved UX : Keeps notifications current and relevant by removing them once the user has acted Risk Mitigation : Prevents scenarios where users might attempt to initiate trades on already-traded inquiries through outdated notification links Feature Requirements The IOI toast notification should automatically dismiss in the following scenarios: 1. Direct Trading via Opportunities Column When a user clicks the Buy or Sell button in the Opportunities column for a row that matches an undismissed IOI message, the system should: Recognize that the user has acknowledged and acted on the IOI Automatically dismiss the IOI toast notification Prevent the notification from reappearing for that inquiry 2. Standard RFQ Submission Flow When a user submits a trade using any submission flow (RFQ, Direct, or Targeted) for an inquiry associated with an undismissed IOI message, the system should: Detect the match between the submitted inquiry and the IOI message Automatically dismiss the IOI toast notification Clean up the notification state

summary: [Canonical] Extend trade  eligibility to US price bonds when subject in Global ASAP
description_text:
Currently in global ASAP, bondlink doesn’t support trade execution of non-EU Price bonds in subject status. When a commingled list with both US/EU in subject status are executed via the Trade All button, the items promptly fail to trade. We need to extend the logic to incorporate US Price bonds. EU price can trade while subject while US price cannot. summary: [BE] PLACEHOLDER Remediation of Viewserver Refactor
description_text: This PR  was completed after support for  $unionWith  was removed in order to support Mongo transactions on the ViewServer. The fix was to allow the viewserver receive a  switch  from capability documents to specify the preference of using  $facet s, which is required for collections that need  $unionWith  (this aggregation stage does not work inside transactions). The switch is functionally similar to a feature flag. The reason why $facet was removed from the pipeline when querying mongo is because this stage is poorly optimized (it generates too many unnecessary documents). The unfortunate side effect of this change is certain capabilities (such as the ones needed for market depth) require facets in order to function properly (see config/xpro/cron-jobs/terraform/manifests/mongo-atlas-maintenance/scripts/eu-asap.js ,  db.createView("market-depth-view", "rfq-market-depth-inventory-eu", [ ). We should review whether the aforementioned  market-depth-view  can function without the  $unionWith or if a change to the service upstream from viewserver is needed so that we can remove this switch.

summary: [FE] Handle Spreadsheet Uploader -> Validation Service -> Spreadsheet Uploader Flow
description_text:
Based on outcome of Spike V1 Spreadsheet Uploader API: V2 Component Docs (pending API docs): We can start development against Spreadsheet Uploader V1 however we cannot close down the Jira and implement step 3 below until V2 is available (linked Jira) and implemented. Once V2 is available we can migrate and handle step 3 although there may be some API breaking changes in steps 1 and 2. Example Spreadsheet (also attached): Full Flow: SSL Summary Display In this Story, there are 3 (annotated above) phases to implement Initiate display of Spreadsheet  Uploader to Activity Console via 2 sceanarios (see attached figma):  TODO: add figma Initiated from an empty AC Via a button on the main bar (when existing RFQs in the AC) Parse data from SSL and generate mutation : Receive the uploaded data in JSON format from the spreadsheet uploader, complete some minor transformations and create GQL mutation  Note: below could change with V2 spreadsheet uploader Example JSON (note array) in the  data  field [{
        Identifier <string>
        Side <string>
        Quantity <number>
        LimitLevel <number> (Optional)
        TargetLevel <number> (Optional)
        Settlement Date <date> (Optional)
        InternalNotes <string> (Optional)
}] Transform this data into GQL request as per  Upload Schema  on Some transformations needed: Sz → Quantity Internal Limit → LimitLevel, Target → TargetLevel Create a new guid for Submission Id Display Validation Summary : Subscribe to summary from backend  rfq-validations  topic and pass back to SSL (V2). Refer to figma for latest UX  . This part is to return the results of validations back to SSL V2. SSL will then handle the display the results for display to user Note, only the handling of the response can be completed at this stage until V2 SSL API is available (however the final API part should be simple and can be done later). For now, we can simply close the V1 SSL via existing V1 intent. For development purposes, can test the flow for s…

summary: [BE][QA] Fix super quickly dealer selection flickering in the UI
description_text: To fix issue with dealer/ot selection when super quickly changing dealer groups: UI to provide along with upstream request payload an UUID, BE to receive process and send this id back, and then when receiving it back from BE into mongo ignore if this id is not latest? e.g.: Problem super quick selection: selection A selection B selection C (last is C) BE-to-Mongo A --> flickering C to A not nice BE-to-Mongo B --> flickering A to B not nice selection D BE-to-Mongo C --> flickering B to C not nice BE-to-Mongo D --> flickering C to D good. Solution: selection A req with selectionId 1 selection B req with selectionId 2 selection C req with selectionId 3 (last is C with id 3) BE-to-Mongo A --> id 1 != 3 -> UI ignores and keep optimistic selection. BE-to-Mongo B --> id 2 != 3 -> UI ignores and keep optimistic selection. selection D req with id 4 (last is D with id 4) BE-to-Mongo C --> id 3 != 4 -> UI ignores and keep optimistic selection. BE-to-Mongo D --> id 4 == 4 -> UI updates selection Technical details: [FE]  to send a unique incremental id: selectionId when sending request payload for dealer selection: -  UIDealerSelection -  UiSmartSelectRequest When selecting “Smart Select” group UI makes 2 calls, to both calls UI should send next selectionId for dealer selection call, and selectionId +1 for SDS, so we can know latest ill be when sds response comes back. e.g.: current selectionId is 2, custom selection is done         - 1st call for dealer selection mutation: send 3         - 2nd call for sds upstream mutation: send 4. Scenario when user did selection and then reload UI: if selectionId in mongo is not null but in redux state is null, UI should set current value in redux state and increment and send for newer selections. [BE]  to receive it and when sending back selection after processing, write this id into mongo

summary: Prod Support - Sprint 27.6 | Apr 30 - May 06
description_text: For any production support request, it's very important we follow this process: You will normally receive communication of the problem, accompanied by an  SD Jira ticket . We should have an engineering (XRFQ) ticket, which has a   relationship to the SD ticket   and this ticket . The engineering ticket should go on your board, with  high priority , marked as a prod support issue. It's very important that we follow up with diagnosing, and solving the problem, or at the very least scheduling a fix. This should be documented in the ticket itself. We must do everything we can to  avoid production support items to be forgotten. Even if your production support rotation ends, we need you to  follow up on open items that were raised during your rotation . If you have any prioritization with your sprint workload, please bring them up during stand up to  Ken Reyes  or  Sam Krickellas .

summary: [CANON] - Spike Retaining Previous Partial Fill Pre-Submission Parameters - Global ASAP
description_text: On Bondlink, after executing upon a partial fill, the new RTS generated retains the parameters of the previous submission. Note - this was never built out for Global ASAP Example: First inquiry (1,000,000) submitted has timer of 5 min, JPM & BARX Selected, Open Trading fully selected This inquiry gets traded at size of 400,000 and new RTS is generated with 600,000 (retaining parameters of timer of 5 min, JPM & BARX Selected, Open Trading fully selected) Second inquiry (from new RTS of 600,000) has parameters changed to 3 min, JPM selected, and Open Trading fully selected This inquiry gets traded at size of 200,000 and new RTS is generated with 400,000 (retaining parameters of timer at 3 min, JPM selected, and Open Trading fully selected) We will need investigative work done to look into implementing the same behavior on Multi-Market list.

summary: [CANON] - Spike Retaining Previous Partial Fill Pre-Submission Parameters - Multi-Market
description_text: On Bondlink, after executing upon a partial fill, the new RTS generated retains the parameters of the previous submission. Example: First inquiry (1,000,000) submitted has timer of 5 min, JPM & BARX Selected, Open Trading fully selected This inquiry gets traded at size of 400,000 and new RTS is generated with 600,000 (retaining parameters of timer of 5 min, JPM & BARX Selected, Open Trading fully selected) Second inquiry (from new RTS of 600,000) has parameters changed to 3 min, JPM selected, and Open Trading fully selected This inquiry gets traded at size of 200,000 and new RTS is generated with 400,000 (retaining parameters of timer at 3 min, JPM selected, and Open Trading fully selected) We will need investigative work done to look into implementing the same behavior on Multi-Market list.

summary: [BE] Client IOI Integration Integration Testing Approach continued
description_text: Please coordinate with   to get a good understanding of the backend feature set being built and the integration patterns for Client IOI. We have three scopes of testing we are trying to achieve for backend modules. Unit Testing : The majority of our tests. Very granular. This falls out of the scope of this exercise, but in general we want to have excellent unit testing across the board with very healthy code coverage. Integration Testing : A medium number of our tests. Focused on transaction sanity. We would love to stand up the specific service(s) that are affected by the change and have a simulator that produce inputs that allow the service to execute the transactions we are trying to test. End-to-End Testing:  A smaller number of tests, focused on asserting integration sanity of system behavior. We would test against a fully integrated live system, with backend dependencies. No simulators. We make sure everything works together as expected. For this second scenario, Integration Testing, we need to find out what patterns are in place in XPro 1.0 to help us perform this kind of exercise, if any. If there aren’t any, and there is no obvious way to move forward in this direction or no history of this kind of exercise in the past, then please let’s focus on how to best enable the End-to-End testing scenario effectively. Are there any operational of system configuration concerns that we would have to address for testing. Is there anything we need to do to trigger transactions? For example, my understanding is that this messaging workflow is initiated by LBA. How do we trigger that on-demand for our testing? What observability to we have in place so we can assert the transaction across the system?

summary: [BE] Incorrect protocol sent to SDS for US HG instrument
description_text: In QA3,  this instrument was sent to SDS in a crossing list: 75513EBZ3 SDS was unable to look up the instrument in the BL database. This is because the protocol sent by XPro was “US Price Protocol”, but in the DB it only had these 2 available: “US Spread Protocol”, “USHG Price Protocol”. Therefore the instrument lookup in SDS failed.

summary: CP + MID Ribbon: Column order does not match Figma design
description_text: The column order in the CP + MID ribbon section does not match the approved Figma design. Expected order (as per Figma): Spread → G-Spread → Price → Yield Actual order (in application): Yield → Price → Spread → G-Spread Because of this mismatch, the UI is inconsistent with the design specifications and may cause confusion for users. Actual Result: Columns are displayed as: Yield, Price, Spread, G-Spread Expected Result: Columns should be displayed in the following order, matching Figma: Spread, G-Spread, Price, Yield

summary: [Dependency - Core2/OT] Targeted Disclosed OT
description_text: Hey Everyone, apologies on raising this one late, but we have 1 last Disclosed OT dependency to add to this list - As we're looking to support  Targeted Disclosed OT Lists  this PI, we're planning to waive off  the  Transaction fees  and  OT fees  when Clients select  1 Disclosed OT Dealer  and trades only with that said Dealer as long as the Dealer is enabled for the existing 'TargetedDOT.NoFee' company prop for the given product segment (HG, HY, EM or EU). This feature  already exists in production for Targeted Disclosed OT Singles  and we will be using the existing company prop and kill-switch for DOT Lists as well. We want to raise a dependency on  all Core teams  to run a basic sanity around fees on their products (Client initiated trade Lists only) to ensure there's no impact. Story links:   ,   , Release: 26.1.0, Sprint: 5 Disclosed OT only: Show Executing Trader details on the API (Singles and Lists) Similar to what we did last PI for DTAPI, we want to now show the executing traders name in CTAPI messages (AI, S, 8, AJ, R) if the  new  company property ‘EnableDisclosedOTInCTAPI’ is enabled, and in Client and Dealer PTMs if the  existing  company property ‘EnableDisclosedOTInPTM’ is set to send the Disclosed OT tags. As we would be touching common code, we would like to raise a dependency on the below Teams to run a basic sanity on their Singles and Lists workflows (both Workstation and API) to ensure no impact.   Story links:    and      Impacted Teams: All Core Teams, Munis and Automation

summary: [BE] Create Remaining Size resulting from Partial Order
description_text: Business Background Client traders enabled with company property  inquiry.MultipleFills  have a unique functionality where new RTS orders are generated as a result of a partially filled order. For example, if my original RTS order size is 1,000M and I execute a partial fill of 250M, then a new RTS order will be created for 750M. The new RTS order should be available in X-Pro 2.0 for the client to submit and trade again.

summary: [Canon] Logging of when Trade proceeds is infinity
description_text: We are seeing another case where the trade proceeds are ending up being Infinity for FF6 trades and this is causing servers to run into issues upon intra-day restarts in Load Test Environment. Here is the query to run: select t.PRINCIPAL, t.PROCEEDS, r.* from inquiry_trade t, inquiry_request r where r.submittime > TO_DATE('2025-12-12','YYYY-MM-DD') and r.inquiryid=t.inquiryid and cast(t.principal as binary_double) = binary_double_infinity. This is causing issues in QA as well as LoadTest.

summary: [Canon] SPIKE - Root Cause of Infinity in Trade Proceeds in LT
description_text: We are seeing another case where the trade proceeds are ending up being Infinity for FF6 trades and this is causing servers to run into issues upon intra-day restarts in Load Test Environment. Here is the query to run: select t.PRINCIPAL, t.PROCEEDS, r.* from inquiry_trade t, inquiry_request r where r.submittime > TO_DATE('2025-12-12','YYYY-MM-DD') and r.inquiryid=t.inquiryid and cast(t.principal as binary_double) = binary_double_infinity. This is causing issues in QA as well as LoadTest.

summary: [BE][FE] Add alternative fields for transient to solve late messages on service recovery
description_text:
If transient service recovers from errors might end up sending late transient messages that can land in DB late and reflect inaccurate state. So adding alternative field and weights should provide UI the ability to pick the state correctly proposed weight list "": 0
Open: 1
CREATING: 2 
DELISTING: 2 
SUBMITTING: 2
CANCELLING: 2
Live: 3
TRADING : 4
PASSING: 4
COUNTERING: 4
Passed: 5
Complete: 5
Cancelled: 5 UI can compare displayStatus and displayStatusTransient to render the higher number.

summary: [BE] Handle trader permissions to allow editing of specific fields (eg RTS_Size)
description_text: List of the client company properties which allow/ disallow editing fields in XPro 2.0: RTS_Size  - when enabled, client is unable to change the Qty (M) field in XPro 2.0 Values can be: D = allow downsizing U = allow upsizing Y = allow all size changes N = all no size changes This is not intraday updateable inquiry.LargestTradeSize.lockSubmit  - when this is enabled, client is unable to edit the Qty (M) field in XPro 2.0 to a size greater than the largest trade size Values in linx are a checkbox This is not intraday updateable RTS_SpotType  - when enabled, this will lock down the spot time of an RTS Values in linx are a checkbox This is not intraday updateable RTS_Size_Targeted  -   When enabled, client is unable to change the size of a targeted inquiry Values can be: D = allow downsizing U = allow upsizing Y = allow all size changes N = all no size changes This is intraday updateable RTS_FxOption  - When enabled, this will lock down the FX Option Values in linx are a checkbox This is not intraday updateable RTS_SettlementDate  - When enabled, the settlement date of an inquiry is locked and is uneditable Values in linx are a checkbox This is not intraday updateable

summary: [FE] Sorting on Main grid not working properly when RFQ status is changed
description_text: When a user opens an RFQ from the Activity Console by double-clicking a row, the RFQ opens in the details panel. After submitting the RFQ, the record’s status changes from  Open  to  Live . Once the status changes, the grid’s automatic sorting logic reorders the records and moves the updated record to a new position. However, the grid still shows the previously selected row as active, even though the actual record has moved. This results in an incorrect row being shown as selected. Steps to Reproduce: Open the  Activity Console . Double-click on any row with status  Open  to open the RFQ in the details panel. Submit the RFQ. Observe the grid after the status changes from  Open  to  Live  and auto-sorting is applied. Expected Result: After submission and auto-sorting, the correct RFQ record should remain selected in the grid (or the selection should be cleared if the record moves).

summary: [Canon] List Level statuses not correctly appearing and showing in XPro 2.0
description_text: 2 issues: When all (or some) items in list are failed, the list status is showing as Open incorrectly - the list should be Complete - virtual list id = 20360870 When all items in list are done, the list status is showing as Open incorrectly - the list should be complete - virtual list id = 20360896 After discussing with Andres, this is because 1 item in the list is being submitted on the US Yield protocol, and not the USHG Price protocol. And therefore it is being removed from the list due to canonical filtering when the list is submitted. Canonical needs to submit this bond on USHG Price and not US Yield protocol, then the item will remain in XPro and the list level statuses will be correct

summary: [Canon] Infinity in Trade Proceeds in LT
description_text: We are seeing another case where the trade proceeds are ending up being Infinity for FF6 trades and this is causing servers to run into issues upon intra-day restarts in Load Test Environment. Here is the query to run: select t.PRINCIPAL, t.PROCEEDS, r.* from inquiry_trade t, inquiry_request r where r.submittime > TO_DATE('2025-12-12','YYYY-MM-DD') and r.inquiryid=t.inquiryid and cast(t.principal as binary_double) = binary_double_infinity. This is causing issues in QA as well as LoadTest.

summary: [BE] Ranking Service Bond Link Responder
description_text: We need to implement or extend existing bond-link responder services to support integration testing the new ranking service that Bryan has been working on. As I understand it, the ranking service does not live by itself, but serves to rank records that are provided by other upstream services. It may be possible that this may not require a net-new bond-link responder service, but may be covered by an existing service, that may or may not need to be updated to validate this scenario. Please do the following: Analyze which transactions the ranking service affects and how it meaningfully changes data or transaction behavior. Analyze how it fits within the kafka topology. Determine if there is an existing bond-link service that covers this. If so, does it require modifications in order to support the changes in behavior introduced by the new service? If not, can we plan out creating a bond-link responder that will cover the scenarios we will need to support not just the ranking service, but the features that it exposes on the frontend. Work with Tim to determine and confirm a playwright testing strategy.

summary: [BE] Refactor dealer-response-levels service
description_text: Now we have a new service called dealer-response-rank service. This service has the responsibility of populating the rank field in the dealer-responses collection for all responses in an inquiry. It also calculates BestLevelSingle fields necessary for single-row collection. Here is a service diagram (blue represents topics, pink represents services, and green represents mongo collections. My service is now calculating all green fields and will messages to a new topic (BestLevel) that contains the best dealer acronym keyed by inquiryid. The response-levels service should consume all responses and join with cpp and cpp close data to calculate diffs for each response (for mongo dealer-responses collection) and best diffs (for single-row collection). (And also dealer level V limit). As part of the refactor, Many classes, schemas, and tests can be deleted completely. In addition to this, the currently implementation only recalculates diffs when responses change, but NOT when cpp changes. This needs to be fixed. PR for new ranking service -

summary: [BE] Client IOI Integration Integration Testing Approach
description_text: Please coordinate with   to get a good understanding of the backend feature set being built and the integration patterns for Client IOI. We have three scopes of testing we are trying to achieve for backend modules. Unit Testing : The majority of our tests. Very granular. This falls out of the scope of this exercise, but in general we want to have excellent unit testing across the board with very healthy code coverage. Integration Testing : A medium number of our tests. Focused on transaction sanity. We would love to stand up the specific service(s) that are affected by the change and have a simulator that produce inputs that allow the service to execute the transactions we are trying to test. End-to-End Testing:  A smaller number of tests, focused on asserting integration sanity of system behavior. We would test against a fully integrated live system, with backend dependencies. No simulators. We make sure everything works together as expected. For this second scenario, Integration Testing, we need to find out what patterns are in place in XPro 1.0 to help us perform this kind of exercise, if any. If there aren’t any, and there is no obvious way to move forward in this direction or no history of this kind of exercise in the past, then please let’s focus on how to best enable the End-to-End testing scenario effectively. Are there any operational of system configuration concerns that we would have to address for testing. Is there anything we need to do to trigger transactions? For example, my understanding is that this messaging workflow is initiated by LBA. How do we trigger that on-demand for our testing? What observability to we have in place so we can assert the transaction across the system?

summary: [BE] Update Service Generation Archetype
description_text: In short:   Add an assertion to our build to check that our service archetype generates valid source. Details:   We do not prevent syntax errors in the archtype used to generate our services as part of the build so we can easily update the archtype and end up with a version on master that will produce new services that will not compile (and possibly confuse new joiners like myself, and in general slow people down who have to manually fix the output).   Fix would be to add an assertion to the build that checks if a newly minted service project generated from the archtype compiles and passes the trivial boilerplate tests that are included with it.  An alternative would be to somehow check the syntax of the archtype template files themselves but this could be more trouble than it’s worth unless there is something off the shelf for it.  There are probably other approaches to this too.

summary: [FE] Handle typeerrors in persisted user column state
description_text: Context: Repo:  maom-ui, shared-ui-components The  columnState ref, which is stored to the persistenceContext, might occasionally get corrupted. Because the state in this ref is relied on for grid initialization, getting that data wrong can lead to runtime exceptions. In the latest such case, a user’s  columnState ref included an undefined value in the colId prop. AgGrid doesn’t accept undefined for the colId prop. As a result, the user encountered a recurring runtime error immediately upon loading the app (specifically, upon the initial load of the Grid). For further details, refer to the linked SD ticket. Technical details: The  Grid  Component in the  shared-ui-components  repo passes in the  handleFirstDataRendered  callback to the  onFirstDataRendered  prop to its agGrid instance. handleFirstDataRendered , which relies on  columnState  ref from the persistenceContext, is invoked once on the initial load of the grid when at least one of the persisted columns has a sort state !== null, the  applyColumnState  api method will be invoked with the columnState agGrid expects a string for colId Runtime errors may propagate when the  columnState  values don’t meet the interface defined by agGrid (e.g. colId === undefined instead of string) Remediation: To address this issue, we propose applying one of the following approaches: Filter out columns that don’t meet the type definition  - in this case, add a filter step to the  columnState  ref values such that colId of undefined cannot get passed into the applyColumnState  invocation. This approach guarantees the state of persisted columnState of all rendered columns but cannot guarantee all columns will be on the grid Fall back to the default column definitions  - in this case, fall back to the default column definitions if a type error is detected This approach guarantees all columns to be on the grid but not in the last persisted state

summary: [FE] Add defensive measures to token fetching
description_text: After the production investigation linked below, we realized that a token call returned with a 200 OK status but with no data. We currently have retry logic if token fetching fails but it only retries depending on the status. So, in the case of the production incident, we tried to fetch the token but since it had a 200 status, we didn't try to fetch again even though it didn’t return any data and kept using the previous token, which ended up expiring. We will add a defensive measure to check the response of the token call and retry if the data is missing in addition to the status check.

summary: Prod Support - Sprint 26.6 | Jan 22 - Jan 29
description_text: For any production support request, it's very important we follow this process: You will normally receive communication of the problem, accompanied by an  SD Jira ticket . We should have an engineering (XRFQ) ticket, which has a  blocking relationship to the SD ticket . It should also have a simple relationship to this ticket. The engineering ticket should go on your board, with  high priority , marked as a prod support issue. It's very important that we follow up with diagnosing, and solving the problem, or at the very least scheduling a fix. This should be documented in the ticket itself. We must do everything we can to  avoid production support items to be forgotten. Even if your production support rotation ends, we need you to  follow up on open items that were raised during your rotation . If you have any prioritization with your sprint workload, please bring them up during stand up to  Nathan Ahdout .

This includes understanding technical challenges, evaluating iframe integration feasibility, and determining what endpoints and functionality need to be exposed. Users need to be able to open and interact with PT list tabs from the 2.0 interface with full functionality. Challenges URL-based tab opening in 1.0 : Currently, there is no functionality to open a tab using a URL in 1.0 - this capability needs to be built Iframe integration : Need to determine if iframe embedding is viable and how authentication would work CPP data updates : CPP data ticking is handled by a worker thread in the 1.0 app - may not work in an iframe context Authentication : Need to solve authentication challenges if using iframe approach Full endpoint exposure : Multiple endpoints beyond just the list view may need to be exposed for complete functionality Goals Evaluate existing iframe integration work and determine if it can be leveraged Test whether CPP worker threads function properly within an iframe context Identify workarounds or alternative solutions if CPP updates don't work in iframes Assess overall viability of iframe approach Determine how authentication would work for iframe-embedded 1.0 content in 2.0 Evaluate token sharing, session management, and security implications Identify potential authentication blockers or limitations Review and document the complete list of endpoints used by PT list tabs in 1.0 Analyze  InquiriesControllerImpl  and related controllers to understand full API surface Design and estimate effort to add URL-based tab opening in 1.0 Identify changes needed to routing/controller layer Questions Is iframe the right approach? What endpoints need to be exposed? What work is needed in 1.0? What work is needed in 2.0? What are the risks and blockers? What’s the estimate for all this work?

summary: [BE] Update Data Model for Market Depth MF
description_text: Catch all ticket to update the data model for Market Depth MF. The table in this  confluence doc  provides an overview of the previous data model (left column -  rfq-md-items-view  and middle column -  summary-panel-view ) and the proposed new model (right column -  rfq-market-depth-inventory-eu ) based on the fields referenced in the client. All of the fields marked with  not present  are what we suggest should be added, but it’s possible that some may be either redundant or no longer needed. They are transcribed below, with details about their types provided in the  confluence doc : protocol rowId shortName instrument tradabilty axeSize totalElements axeCount We also need to update (or verify) the  instrumentId  field so that returns a  string | number  instead of just the  number .  According to senior product leadership , ISINs and CUSIPs can be alphanumeric. The  instrument  field appears to be CP+ data and may require its own query. We’d like to avoid joining collections into a single view for performance reasons. We should consider exposing this as a direct query parameterized by  instrumentId .

[Canon] US Spread - Support Spot Type submitted from XPro 2.0 UI
description_text: When XPro 2.0 submits a Multi-Market RFQ List including HG Spread bonds, the list request provides an applicable spot type such as 1-Step, Spot Later, Standard, 3PM Spot, etc. The delayed spot types such as 11AM Spot, 3PM Spot, etc. are setup on a per company basis using the company property  Inquiry.3PMScheduleTag . The list request submitted by XPro needs to be supported in BondLink. The list items need to be set to appropriate spot type based on the trading protocol for the item. HG Spread protocol items can have a spot type of Standard, 1-Step, or one of available delayed spot types for the company, whereas the Yield protocol items can only support a spot type of 1-Step. Voleon, and some other clients may be submitting RTS with Auto spot type as 5626=3. Ensure orders on lists with that tag/spot type are not getting rejected after this change.

summary: Incorrect Netted Hedge Qty with incorrect side when the same benchmark belongs to Cross off and cross on inquiries
description_text: Steps to reproduce: Push a list where some inquiries have cross off and some have cross on Submit the list by selecting same benchmark Respond from the dealer and trade the list Expected: It should consider the Netted Hedge Qty as the difference between Buy size and sell size If the difference is positive, the Side should be Buy and if the difference is Negative, it should be Sell Actual: It only added the cross on hedge values and then subtracted the cross off value even though all are Buy (6555 + 95)-295 = 6355 which is considered as Netted Hedge Qty for the given benchmark in the screenshot Side displays as Sell where the difference is positive 6555 + 95 + 295 = +2945 should be Netted Hedge Qty with Buy side for 912810UN6 Benchmark Cusip

summary: Side incorrectly displayed for Cross OFF inquiries under Net Hedge Treasury Grid
description_text: Steps to reproduce: Send a list having mostly spread bonds Submit the list with Cross OFF Respond from the dealer end and complete the trade Expected: Side should be Buy when aggregated Netted Hedge Qty is Positive, and Side should be Sell when aggregated Netted Hedge Qty is Negative Actual: For Cross OFF inquiries → Side displayed as Buy when aggregated Netted Hedge Qty is Negative, and Sell when aggregated Netted Hedge Qty is Positive As shown in below screenshot-> for the benchmark cusip 91282CPC9→  Sell Size (25)- Buy Size (130+170) = 25 - 300 = -275 The agg. Netted hedge is negative so the side should be Sell for given benchmark. But it displays as Buy side. The issue exists for all the benchmark in the grid. Cross off → 1-step → Side is incorrect Cross Off → Delayed spot

Direct RFQ: Cross is not working when turns on "Cross" from Direct modal popup: Default cross is OFF
description_text: Cross is not working as expected when turns on "Cross" from Direct modal popup. Issue 1: Pre-condition: Default cross is OFF Steps to reproduce: send single RFQ and click on Buy/Sell button from modal popup Enable cross on from modal popup Submit the direct inquiry Issue: Cross is Off/disable on X-Pro as well as on dealer responds screen. Acceptance criteria:  Cross should be enabled after submitting the direct trade and consistent throughout the trade. summary: 401 Page in absence of credentials
description_text: Milan Bhatia  has requested that the  xpro-1   maom-ui  application produce a HTTP 401 response when no credentials are present, and that the main screen of the application does not render. The user can be redirected to an error page indicating that no credentials are present. When the application is loaded directly in a browser environment, there is no request for authentication. This can be verified by loading the staging URL in a browser. Please note: I was not able to observe any attempts to communicate with the backend, no web socket connection was opened, no REST calls were made. However, this is still considered undeseriable from a security perspective. A non-authenticated user should not be able to see the main screen of the application at all. Ideally they are redirected to a page that indicates they don’t have an active session, prompting the user to follow steps for re-authentication. It’s important when this change is made, that we ensure this is not disruptive to development and testing workflows, which still happen directly in a browser, and not necessarily through JX Browser or the workstation.

summary: [Testing] PI26 EU-ASAP Playwright
description_text: PI26 Xpro RFQ Playwright backlog & maintenance Scope: TBC We need help from   in understand the scope of work to complete the coverage gaps for EU ASAP. As part of this exercise, Adam has proposed he would do the following: Refine the collection of test cases that currently sit on TestRail for EU ASAP There are many test cases that don’t fit automation cleanly. There are a lot of duplicates. Adam has a ball park estimate of about 1 out of 3 test cases will be usable. With a total universe of about 164 test cases, that gives us a possible collection of 50-60 test cases in total to implement Create an easy way to filter, indentify or reorgnize these test cases, to make it easy for the team to pick them up and work on them. Opertional stability for testing Help ensure the team has a working environment where test cases can be executed manually and in an automated fashion. This will help avoid false positives and help the team actually validate their implemented flows work correctly. Testing prerequisites Suffix users to be able to inject messages. Understanding of and access to  bond-link responder  and its workflows to help support testing efforts. An avenue for our team to report opertional or software issues on the platform and get support on a reasonable response time. Perhaps   can be invited to EU ASAP stand up, and coordinate any blockers or issues on behalf of the team. Progress reporting We need to create Jira items for all the pruned test cases we want to consider implementing. It doesn’t need to be a 1-to-1 relationship between test case and Jira ticket. These can be grouped together by testrail group or test suite. ✅ We need to create a dashboard or Jira Structure that shows progress for these test cases. It should be easy for someone like Chris or Shane to get an up-to-date understanding of where we are with test case development, how much work is left and what is our rate of progress.

summary: Net Cross RFQ [DEV 8; QA 8]
description_text: Pre Submission If ‘ Inquiry.RFQNetCrossSubmitter ' is enabled at the company level then the users in XPro should have access to the Net Cross option when submitting an RFQ list. The option should only exist if the company property and the system property are enabled inquiry.enableRFQNetCross. The system property is the master switch which overrides the company level property. If System = Y AND Company = Y, users in company have Net Cross option If System = Y AND Company = N, Users in company do NOT have Net Cross option If System = N AND(Company = Y OR Company = N), users in company do NOT have Net Cross option. If the user is eligible to see the Net Cross option then show another toggle in the Type window below Cross All. Net Cross All will appear as a toggle similar to Cross All. Submit list to BL as Net Cross (check which field needs to be set in BL)# Net Cross can only be ON or OFF for all spread items in list. Not possible to turn on a few items only or remove a few items if rest are Net Cross. All or None style. Settle Date must be consistent across all orders in the Net Cross list, change settle date on one order  will change for all. Aggregate Panel Netted Benchmark values show in aggregated hedge panel PTM Validate PTMs for Net Cross specific values Out of scope Exclude changing BM Settle date from scope estimate

description_text: View only trade manager users can only view the orders of other traders Enhance trade manager users can take over inquiries from other traders Scenarios for view-only TM: If trader has a mixed list of different market segments, and the TM is permissioned to view at least one of the items in the list. Then TM should be able to view the entire list. Scenarios for enhanced TM: If trader has a mixed list of different market segments, and the TM is not permissioned to edit at least one of the items in the list. Then TM should  not be able to edit  the list. But the TM can still  view  the list. If trader submits a mixed list of different market segments to dealers A, B and C, and the TM only has a relationship with dealer A. Then TM should  not be able to edit  the list. But the TM can still  view  the list.

summary: Spring-Boot / Netty Version Update
description_text: Spring Boot We need to update to using  Spring Boot 3.5.6  which has a transitive dependency of  Spring Framework 6.2.11 . This version of the framework addresses a known security vulnerability.  Our builds will break until we address this vulnerability . We have been granted a security exception, which is set to expire on Oct 10th, at which point our builds will go back to breaking. We need to upgrade the dependency, and address any breaking changes or unwanted side effects caused by the upgrade. We also need to support the testing cycle to mitigate any possible regressions caused by the upgrade. According to external resources the main risks are around HTTP redirect behavior changes and actuator endpoint access controls. References https://marketaxess.atlassian.net/jira/servicedesk/projects/SD/queues/issue/SD-13061 Netty Netty is simpler as it’s a direct dependency. It needs to be updated to  4.1.124.Final  to mitigate the reported CVE. This is a fix release upgrade. If they respect semantic versioning, in theory this upgrade should not introduce any breaking changes. It should be safer to make this upgrade directly. References

summary: Component Resizing and Responsive Breakpoints
description_text: We have a problem with the application’s user interface lack of responsiveness in distinct breakpoints. When in a smaller horizontal breakpoint, the main panel does not respond to resizing events. it’s not possible for the user to adjust the horizontal distribution between main and right panels. In a small horizontal breakpoint, the size distribution between main and detail panels is such that the user cannot read records from the main panel. It looks like the detail panel has a fixed size, that does not respond well to smaller breakpoints. In a larger breakpoint, it is possible for the user to resize the main panel, and also possible to read records. Traders have very particular configurations around screen distribution, and tend to have very specific patterns to maximize screen real-estate. We need to make sure that the application follows responsive design strategies, and ensure it’s usable at different breakpoints. The simplest thing we can do in the short term is  ensure the main panel remains resizeable, even in a small horiontal breakpoint . Additionally, it would be nice if there was a more proportial distribution of space, relative to the window size, so that the main panel isn’t constrained to begin with. Issue Demonstration Video Recording:

The blue highlighted rows indicate the actions available for bulk actions upon selecting individual inquiries. **pending business discussion New Order(s) can be newly staged RTS, remainder partial RTS, or RTS on DNT’d (if permissioned). ASAP Available Actions Pre-Submit [New Order(s)] Cancel, Create List Post-Submit (No Responses) Cancel Post-Submit (Responses In) Pass, Trade, Counter** Post Trade Cancel Trade (Different from cancelling inquiry)** Bin Available Actions Pre-Submit [New Order(s)] Cancel, Create List Post-Submit Pending Bin Cancel Post-Submit Post Bin Release (No Responses) N/A - DNT Post-Submit Post Bin Release (Responses In) Pass, Trade, Counter** Post Trade Cancel Trade (Different from cancelling inquiry)** Search, Filtering and Sorting The user will be able to search the main grid for keywords The user will be able tp search each column for keywords The user will be able to filter the main grid for order attributes (specific order attributes will be denoted in the story) The user will be able to sort each column in Asc or Desc order

summary: PROD Hulk not consuming from all partitions of pretrade kafka due to AZs issue
description_text: In prod we have consumer lag on few partitions of  Pretrade kafka topic  due to the below error in hulk  logs [Consumer clientId=consumer-pretrade-xpro-consumer-prod-prd-10, groupId=pretrade-xpro-consumer-prod-prd] Connection to node 5 (lkc-nk1pyz-g005-use1-az1-p5955z.us-east-1.aws.glb.confluent.cloud/10.78.102.94:9092) terminated during authentication. This may happen due to any of the following reasons: (1) Authentication failed due to invalid credentials with brokers older than 1.0.0, (2) Firewall blocking Kafka TLS traffic (eg it may only allow HTTPS traffic), (3) Transient network issue. Impact: High touch column population will have latency issues, also PT dealer axes flow will be impacted. Sharing the below from Pretrade team upon investigation. We are integrating a Confluent Cloud cluster on AWS with a Private Link endpoint on our AWS account. Confluent Cloud is deployed in AZs: az1, az4, az6. Our Private Link VPC endpoint is in AZs: az4, az6. Route 53 Private Hosted Zone (PHZ) for the VPC endpoint only resolves in: az2, az4, az6. Problem: AWS AZs are not consistent across accounts. If Confluent's az1 ≠ your az2, az4, or az6, then brokers in Confluent's az1 won't be able to connect to the Private Link endpoint. Service instances that connect to Confluent's az1 with Private Link endpoint have failures and some of the partitions are never consumed. Solution: Add a subnet in that AZ (az1) to your VPC endpoint. Note:  Can’t be reproduced or verified in QA env’s as it’s single AZ, issue applicable to only mult AZ envs. We need to follow up with the SRE team to solve the cross-availability-zone connectivity issue. We have connection error logs and a dashboard that clearly show hundreds of thousands of messages to be consumed.
