summary: Dealer Responses and Countering
description_text: Business Background Countering is an essential functionality that allows for bilateral pricing on the RFQ protocol at MarketAxess. This ensures the party (initiator) and counterparty (responder) traders can negotiate the price on an inquiry back and forth until agreed, a participant passes or negotiation time expires. Acceptance Criteria Functionality Requirements The Party trader can counter any one of the Counterparties in the response stack with a Level and Good For Timer (with the exception for EU  see note below ) For ASAP RFQ the action can occur as soon as responses are available For BIN RFQ the action can occur after bin release If the Party counters worse than the best level, US and EM: a soft warning will be issued informing the party they are countering worse than the best level if RestrictToExecuteBestResponseUS and/or RestrictToExecuteBestResponseEM is enabled, hard stop per market segments EU: a hard stop is in place if countering worse than the best level Please ensure the proper level checks are in place for ASAP as levels change at a faster rate If the Party counters equal to or better than the Counterparty’s level, no speedbump necessary and counter can be submitted All other responses in the stack will change to Subject once a counter is initiated for US and EM inquiries EU inquiries, the responses will remain Live as long as the GFT has not expired If the Counterparty passes, then the Party can counter another response in the stack until list timer is complete If the Counterparty times out, then the Party can counter another response in the stack until list timer is complete If the Counterparty counters back, then refer to bullet point 2 and 3 The Party should NOT be able to Buy/Sell a response which is Subject for US and EM Products, they must Counter The Party can Buy/Sell a response which is Subject for EU Products, it will ask counterparty to refresh levels The Counterparty statuses will change based on specific events outlined below If the P…

PI27 - xpro1 | Client IOI Continued
description_text: Business Background The Client IOI project is intended to capture uncommitted orders sitting on client third party platforms by linking matching Axe opportunities. Functionality How does the matching work? Clients will send IOI (35=6) messages to LBA with their uncommitted order details. If a matching Axe opportunity (meaning opposite side of the client’s IOI) is submitted by a dealer, LBA will send an acknowledgement of the match (35=8). The acknowledgement message will have an IOIID the client can use to link their single order message (35=D) to MarketAxess. MarketAxess will display the single order message as a client IOI opportunity along with an alert and opportunities column indicator. The submission will be built on top of the Direct RFQ workflow which will allow the client to engage the dealer with an executable firm limit which will auto-execute if the dealer responds at or better than the limit. Important Features to Note The dealer will be notified the workflow is a Direct RFQ. The Direct RFQ fee discounts will apply. Auto-execution will occur if the dealer response is equal to or better than the executable limit. The inquiry_discountschedule table houses all Direct RFQ discounts. It can be distinguishes by type='DRTRFQ.' In production we have 100% discounts on single dealer Direct RFQs. The following tags must be in the Quote Request (35=R) to indicate a Direct RFQ. 29908-DealerAxed: Y → currently enabled through  SendIfAxed  dealer company property 29927-MKTXRFQType: Direct (D) → currently enabled through  EnableRFQType  dealer company property The client will have the ability to edit the executable limit (but initially tethered to the dealer axe level). The client will have the ability to negotiate if the dealer responds worse than the executable limit. Figma Designs

summary: Client IOIs
PI27 - Bin RFQ Post Submission
description_text: Business Background Compared to an ASAP RFQ, a holding bin RFQ provides the dealers with a specified time period to respond before the client can action. Ultimately, there are no significant advantages to Bin vs ASAP trading. The benefits stem from having control over when prices are available and giving dealers more time to send levels. Ultimately up to the trader’s preferences as to when they can hit/lift levels. However, there are additional spotting and hedging functionalities available for High Grade bonds which are valuable for our clients. Bin Scope Bin RFQs in X - Pro 2.0 will be initiated as  Multi-Market  lists. As a result, the market segments in scope are  US High Grade ,  US High Yield ,  US Agencies , and  Emerging Markets Hard Currency . Clients can submit Bin RFQs as  1-Step  or  Delayed Spot . Clients can also choose to submit the inquiry as  Outright  (or Spot) or they can  Cross  with the liquidity provider.  The max number of orders a client can submit in a list will be determined by X. Protocol Scope Spread, Yield, Price and Discount Margin  (similar to Spread) can be traded in Bin RFQs. The order will have protocols assigned to each instrument. Submission and Trading After submitting the lists, a  Due In/Due At  timer (system properties by default, user preference if set) will kick off after which the client will begin to receive responses. The levels will be revealed (based on company property) during bin by default. Once the Due In timer expires, the levels will release and client can take the following actions:  trade ,  counter , or  pass . Delayed Spot Delayed spot options on submission are pre-determined by company property (ex:  11 AM ,  1 PM ,  3 PM ,  4 PM ) along with  Spot Later . Spot Later is technically a spot at 4:45 PM (which can be changed by system property). Clients will also have the ability to  Amend  the delayed spot time to either  Spot Now  or any of the valid times left in the day (ex: originally 4 PM spot, client can c…

summary: PI27 - Multiple Partial Fills
description_text: Business Background Multiple partial fills allows clients to trade remaining order size if their original order trades as a partial. Functionality A client’s original order size may not be fully executed. In the event they execute a partial fill, the remaining size of the order will appear as a new order on the activity console. Ideally, details from the original order remain such as dealer selections and other parameters. The remaining order can be added to a new list or submitted as a single order. inquiry.MultipleFills  → must be enabled to allow multiple fills inquiry.RTS_Size  → restrictions on RTS quantity for LT client Product Scope US and EM Hard Currency products only. EU Products are not in scope. List Type Scope Supported for ASAP and Bin Half Moon Icons Half moon icons will be displayed on orders related to a partial fill. Including the following scenarios: Single Partial Filled Orders Initial partial fill half moon tool tip will indicate partial fill and original size along with the counterparty Subsequent partial fills will indicate the most recent partial fill and most recent submitted size along with the counterparty (and parent order information?) Lists where at least one order has partially filled Hover over half moon will display tool tip indicating “X items traded with a partial quantity” Remaining Orders from partial fills (MPF enabled) Hover over half moon will show tooltip message indicating the parent order/previous order Persisting Parameters The remaining partial fills should maintain the original parameters from the last order submitted including: Due In/Good For timers Disclosed Dealer Selections Market List Selections Side/Direction Limit Spot Time Spot Type PTM Requirements The Original Quantity, Last Quantity and Remaining Quantity tags must have the correct quantity as fills occur. Direct Bug: Counter worse popup is being displayed when countering with same level as dealer level for Direct rfq Sell inquiry for Yield bond
description_text: When attempting to counter a Direct sell inquiry for a yield bond at the same level as the dealer's current level, the system incorrectly triggers the "Counter Worse" validation pop-up. This behavior is unexpected because the counter level is not worse than the dealer's level—it is the same. Steps to reproduce: Send Direct buy inquiry with limit 8.55 on yield bond - 63938CAJ7 Dealer responds with level 9.5 Client counter with same level what dealer provides ( eg. 9.501 ) Issue:  The "Counter Worse" pop-up appears even when the counter level matches the dealer's level. Expected Behavior: If the counter level is equal to the dealer's level, the system should not display the "Counter Worse" pop-up. The pop-up should only appear when the counter level is truly worse than the dealer's level. Observations: Tested same scenario with RFQ, Direct and Process Trade flow on sell side. It’s not throwing error when client counters with same level as dealer’s. For RFQ: For Targeted: For Process Trade:

description_text: When attempting to counter a targeted buy inquiry for a yield bond at the same level as the dealer's current level, the system incorrectly triggers the "Counter Worse" validation pop-up. This behavior is unexpected because the counter level is not worse than the dealer's level—it is the same. Steps to reproduce: Send targeted buy inquiry with limit 8.55 on yield bond - 24422EVK2 Dealer responds with level 7.5 Client counter with same level what dealer provides ( eg. 7.495 ) Issue:  The "Counter Worse" pop-up appears even when the counter level matches the dealer's level. Expected Behavior: If the counter level is equal to the dealer's level, the system should not display the "Counter Worse" pop-up. The pop-up should only appear when the counter level is truly worse than the dealer's level. Observations: Tested same scenario with RFQ, Direct and Process Trade flow on buy. It’s not throwing error when client counters with same level as dealer’s. For RFQ For Process Trade For Direct

summary: Countering Worse than pop-up looking at limit intead of level  (Q: 5,)
description_text: In prod ibarnes1 submitted a counter level better than the best level for USHB price and received this pop-up: Issue 1: We do not have any validation logic for this countering pop-up in place for US Hybrid Price. Current behaviour: countering with a level that is worse or better than the best level results in the above pop-up ibarnes1 also submitted a counter level better than the best level but the same as his limit level for a Price bond and received the same pop-up Issue 2: We are validating countering with the same logic used for trade(hit lift) i.e. checking against limits so that if the counter level is in between the limit & the best level the pop-up appears. We need to re-work the countering logic so that the countering level is only being compared to the best level. This then needs to be applied to USHB price also.

summary: [Canon] US Spread - Set list type to Multi-Market for Lists sent via FIX
description_text: Summary This issue involves implementing a method to set the list type to Multi-market for lists sent via FIX. Currently, the default behavior breaks down the list into market segment types unless the user selects a specific preference. Context The current system relies on user preferences to determine the list type. This leads to complications as it supports five separate BL list types. The goal is to standardize the behavior for XPro users. Acceptance criteria Implement a way to force the list type to Multi-market when an E message is sent via FIX. Remove reliance on user preferences for setting the list type. Ensure that the new behavior applies to all XPro users. Supported procotols on MM list type US Spread Protocol 1 US Yield Protocol 3 USHB Price Protocol 40 US Price Protocol 2 US DM Protocol 4

summary: [FE] Bring dealer response grid in line with main grid next actions
description_text: Dependent on  https://marketaxess.atlassian.net/browse/EUX-2050 DISCOVERY What are the possible nextAction values? PARTYACCEPT, PARTYCOUNTER, PARTYPASS What are the nextActions values associated with each instrument type in the following scenarios? Submitted but not yet received Received Inquiry Good for timer is active Dealer Good for timer is active Dealer Good for timer is expired Inquiry Good for timer is expired Dealer Good for timer exceeds the Inquiry Good for timer Never received and inquiry good for timer expires Does the nextActions value correctly omit PARTYACCEPT for non-best scenarios? EU Price Company restriction TECHNICAL DETAILS the nextActions values should be sourced from the dealer response collection in mongo useViewserverTable to pull data and filter by rowId actions button container should be pinned right, have a cell for each dealer and initialize with disabled buttons buttons should be enabled once dealer response is received and actionable this pinned right container should disappear after the transaction flow is completed the dealer response col defs should handle changes dynamically options: use context dynamically generate colDefs at runtime with function There shouldn’t be the need to handle sort on FE, full reliance on new ranking service Data needed for the data response grid come from: xpro-activity-console-bl rfq-single-eu-view rfq-dealer-responses-eu-view SAMPLES EU = EU Price → EUR Eurobond/GBP Eurobond/EUR High Yield/GBP High Yield (are the main ones these are High Grade and High Yield Eurobonds but all the ones in EU tab are going to trade EU Price) US = USHG Price on Price -> US Domestic Corp (must be enabled with enableUSHGonPrice company property to use this protocol bc it by default trades on Spread) US Price → US High Yield/US High Yield Distressed USHB Price → US Hybrid EM = US Price → Emerging Markets Price
